text.title: django-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for django-testing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Configure pytest-django via pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options]. Set DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE, addopts with --strict-markers, and testpaths = ["tests"]. Use @pytest.mark.django_db (savepoint rollback) for all model and service tests by default. Use @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True) only for code that calls on_commit or uses raw transactions.
rule.rationale: Centralizing pytest config in pyproject.toml eliminates pytest.ini / setup.cfg duplication. The default django_db marker wraps each test in a savepoint and rolls back after — zero DB state leaks, fast execution. transaction=True commits real transactions but requires full truncation after each test, making it 5-10x slower. It is only needed for post_save signals or Celery tasks that check committed state.

rule.statement: Put shared fixtures in tests/conftest.py: api_client, authed_client, staff_client, and factory registrations. Use --reuse-db in local development to skip recreating the test database. Use --create-db to force recreation after schema changes.
rule.rationale: A thin conftest with only shared infrastructure loads fast and remains readable. --reuse-db skips the migrate step when the schema has not changed, reducing test suite startup from 10-30s to under 1s on large projects. It is safe for all tests using @pytest.mark.django_db (rollback isolation).

rule.statement: Create exactly one Factory Boy factory per model. Register factories in tests/conftest.py using pytest_factoryboy.register(). This auto-creates model (single instance) and model_factory (the factory class) pytest fixtures. Use SubFactory for relationships, Faker for realistic data, LazyAttribute for derived fields.
rule.rationale: One factory per model with Trait variants prevents divergence (one factory creates a "valid" user, another creates a different user with different defaults). pytest_factoryboy.register auto-creates two fixtures per factory, eliminating manual fixture definitions. SubFactory and Faker make factories self-maintaining as schemas evolve.

rule.statement: Use factory.LazyAttribute(lambda o: ...) to derive field values from other fields on the same factory (username from email). Use factory.LazyFunction(lambda: ...) for independent computed values (unique emails, timestamps).
rule.rationale: LazyAttribute receives the object being constructed, allowing derived values like username = LazyAttribute(lambda o: o.email.split('@')[0]). LazyFunction is for values that don't depend on other fields but must be computed at factory call time.

rule.statement: Use DRF's APIClient for all REST endpoint tests. Create api_client, authed_client, and staff_client fixtures in conftest.py. Never instantiate APIClient() inside individual test functions. Use client.force_authenticate(user=user) in tests — not credentials(HTTP_AUTHORIZATION='Token...'). force_authenticate bypasses token validation and tests the view logic, not the token verification.
rule.rationale: Centralizing APIClient creation in fixtures ensures consistent configuration across the test suite. force_authenticate is faster and decouples view tests from the auth backend implementation — changing from JWT to OAuth2 does not break view tests. Token verification is tested once in dedicated auth tests.

rule.statement: Assert status code first, then assert on response.data fields individually. Never assert only on response.status_code == 200 — verify the response shape is correct. Always use format='json' in requests, never rely on browsable API HTML.
rule.rationale: A 200 with an empty body or missing fields is a bug. Asserting on response.data['field'] catches serializer field omissions and field name renames. format='json' enforces API contract instead of HTML rendering.

rule.statement: Use @pytest.mark.parametrize to test permission matrices: which roles/states produce which HTTP status codes. One parametrized test is better than N separate tests with identical structure.
rule.rationale: Permission logic is combinatorial — a ViewSet with 4 HTTP methods and 3 user roles requires 12 test cases. Parametrization makes the matrix explicit and readable. Adding a new role only requires a new tuple in the parametrize list, not a new test function.
